Hey everyone, this is Russell and welcome you guys to today's Funnel Hack training.
I'm going to make sure you guys can see my screen and you can hear me.
Our last training, I went for like 10 minutes before my screen was showing.
So all right, cool.
You guys can hear it.
All right, sweet.
We've got an awesome group today too, which is exciting.
And there's a lot of you guys on here.
Sweet.
All right, so I'm excited for today's training.
As you got, if you got my email, you saw we kind of are doing things a little bit out of order today because the automated webinar funnels, we've been working on one of our own automated webinars.
And as you will notice this inside of ClickFunnels, as we need something, we start playing with it and then we're like, oh, what if we do this and this and this.
And so over the next week to a week or so, we've got a bunch of new updates coming out with automated webinars.
And so I wanted to wait until we have those because they're a lot of them are a big deal and will dramatically impact your success with them.
And so I didn't want to like rush this one now and then come back later.
And so that's kind of the game plan.
I thought, you know, this week, let's move ahead.
We'll do membership sites and then we'll move back and do automated webinars as soon as the we got some of these new features and stuff in there.
So is that all right with you guys, hopefully?
All right, cool.
So today's the membership sites and I'm excited for this because just kind of a backstory.
I've been doing this business now for about 12 years and it's funny because I've had a lot of ups and downs as I'm sure you guys have.
And as an entrepreneur, I don't normally share with my audience the downs, but you know, we've had downs in our company.
And I remember when I first got started, it was just me and I was doing these things and I was selling stuff online and people were buying stuff and it was so exciting.
And then a little while later, I was kind of lonely.
I don't know if you guys ever feel this as an entrepreneur where you're kind of when you're kind of, you know, you have people to talk to you, no one really understands you and you know, you're working, you know, 80 hours a week, making $0 an hour because you believe in something.
You got a vision and a dream and it was just, it was hard to relate to other people when I was going through this.
And so I remember I was kind of lonely and one day one of my friends, he got excited about what I was doing and he's like, man, this is cool.
I want to do this.
And he asked me questions about it.
And I was so excited.
I was like, what if I just hired him that way and have somebody here that could talk to me about this all day long.
So I ended up hiring him and then I hired some of my other friends and eventually I had five or six of my friends working for me.
And it was funny because they didn't know what they were doing.
And I, you know, I'd been hustling and making some money, but I didn't know what they, what they wanted to do either.
And you know, I didn't know how to train them or how to, I had no idea what was happening.
And so it was really, really tough.
And what was interesting that I found out when you start growing a business and you have employees and things like that, that while as an entrepreneur, you know, that you get paid when you sell something.
But employees like to get paid every two weeks, whether they sell anything or not.
And it became this really weird thing where like they come say, Hey, we need to paycheck.
And I'm like, we haven't sold anything.
Like, well, I still got to get paid.
That's how it works.
And so I had to like pay them and then two weeks later they weren't going to get paid again.
So I started going into this, this reactive mode where I would lock my door and try to sell stuff and try to do things all day just to pay payroll.
And then these guys were out there wondering what they, you know, I should, what they should do.
And it was just, it was, it was the reason why an entrepreneur shouldn't manage businesses basically.
So that was kind of the struggle I was going through.
And, and I remember that this little business I had got the point where almost it literally almost went bankrupt.
I was the end of it.
It was actually Christmas time.
And I remember I was outside.
I was freezing cold outside.
I was hanging up Christmas lights around my house and had the most sick feeling in my stomach where I was just like, I have to let go all my friends tomorrow.
Cause I've got, I've got no way to pay payroll.
Like I've got, I've got nothing.
And I was thinking about like, what can I do?
How can I save this?
How can I, I need something.
And so one of the ideas that came to my head and I'd seen some people do this, I'd saw Dan Kennedy and Bill Glazer's company do this.
They had what they call the continuity program or membership program where people sign up and they pay you each month.
And I thought, you know, if we had something like that, it would give us some stability where every single month we had money coming in.
And so we step back and we started figuring out how can we create some continuity income.
And I had this idea.
We put it together in a night because literally it was like the next day I was going to have to fire everyone.
So instead I had called them up.
I had an idea.
Come on in the office.
We had to figure this thing out.
And, and we launched this little funnel and the funnel was giving away a free DVD, which was a two hour presentation of me speaking.
And then, and then with that, they got a free 30 day trial to this newsletter I'd created, this print newsletter.
And then after that, 30 days later, if they wanted to stay on it, then we'd build them $30 a month or maybe it was $40 a month.
Anyway, whatever it was, we did that.
So put a whole campaign together.
We launched it.
And in the very first month, we sold 800 of these DVDs, which meant we had 800 people that were on this, this continuity program.
Um, and so what's the math on them?
I'm not the mathematician in the room.
So you got 800 people times 40 bucks, 32,000.
So 30 days later, excuse me, we made some money because we had like some upsells after the DVDs and we ended up calling people and selling coaching and a couple of other things like that, which, which got us so we could, I didn't have to fire everyone during Christmas, which was cool.
But the most exciting thing is 30 days later, boom, the rebills hit.
And so like I said, the math of my calculator here.
So $32,000 came through.
Um, and I was a little lower in that cause obviously we had some cancellations and stuff, but $32,000 came through the next month and it came through.
Remember it was January came, it came to the office and boom, $32,000 came in about a week where I didn't have to do anything new for that.
I didn't come up with a new idea.
Excuse me.
I didn't come up with a new product or just, it just came through and we had $30,000 and it paid for my payroll.
It gave me a nice chunk of money.
And then anything else we did that month on top of that was just, was just gravy, it was just free money.
And, uh, and I remember thinking, man, like, how did I run a business before I had continuity?
Like, I have no idea how I even survived.
I don't want anyone survives without some kind of continuity.
And I remember I was talking to, uh, one of my, my friends, one of my mentors, his name's David Frye, and we were talking about this concept of like continuity.
And he, uh, this is probably four or five years ago.
He said that, uh, his new year's resolution was I need to focus on how to get more continuity in my business.
And that was his entire driving focus for a year.
And they said something after that, that, that had a huge impact on me.
He says, you know, if you don't have continuity and you don't have a business, um, I started thinking about that.
You know, that's, that's so true.
Like it changes your, your ability to, to perform and do things when you know that every month you wake up and there's X amount of dollars coming in the bank account before the month even starts.
And if you don't have that, then you're literally living by the seat of your pants.
I was talking to my friends today.
And, uh, they literally launched a product.
Uh, I would say at least once a month, sometimes twice a month.
I was like, man, why do you have launched so much stuff?
I'm like, you need to build, uh, like you need to change this away from a launch business where you're just throwing out ideas and doing things and transform it into a continuity business where you've got some stability and, uh, and, and, uh, and you can actually grow a good, a good business.
And so that's kind of what this, this training is about is, is for you guys.
It's cause I want you guys being successful.
I want you guys having the cash flow you need cause cash flows king as we know in business and, uh, like my friend, David Fry said, that if you don't have continuity in your business and you don't have a business, so that's what this is about.
Now, when we first created a click funnels, we kind of went back and forth.
Like, should we create membership sites or not?
Or should we just do sales funnels?
Like how should we do this?
And, and it came down to like realizing that like one of the staples everyone needs is, is a membership site.
Even if they're selling one off products, you still need a place to house these products.
Have somebody buys, they can log in, they can download it, they can get all that stuff.
And so, um, and so we decided to go and, and create membership sites.
And so, um, I'm really excited.
I'm really proud of the membership site, uh, bill that we have inside click farms.
We've been using it like crazy.
Um, and, uh, and so we're going to be showing you guys that today.
But, uh, but before we do that, I wanted to just get part of these calls.
I like to, to focus on the strategy because, um, because again, I think that's important for you guys is to think about the strategy, not just, you know, how to, how to execute it.
Cause the strategy is important.
If you get the strategy right, uh, it makes it a lot easier than, uh, then if you get the strategy wrong.
In fact, in our high-end coaching programs, um, when everyone comes in, um, usually within the first two weeks or so, I get on the phone with that, with everyone for an hour and I look at the business, I try to get the strategy right.
Cause if you have the, the right strategy, you can, you can run in the right direction and be successful.
If you have the wrong strategy, it doesn't matter how fast you're running and how hard you're trying or how many hours a day you're putting in.
If you run in the wrong direction, you're never going to get to where you want to go.
And so that's why we do those, those strategy things.
And that's why we're focusing on the strategy here as well for you guys.
So, so this is on the strategy.
So I'm going to talk about two, two kind of core strategies.
One's a membership site and, uh, and one's a called a micro continuity site.
There's some cool, um, things about both of these that they're important.
So I'm going to talk about those.
So first thing is that I am about to launch a new continuity program and it's a part membership site, part newsletter, and I'm really excited for it.
And so, um, this is something that I'm launching here.

I did this all by myself for the most part.
I had a little help on a couple of pieces, but this is the continuity funnel.

Okay.
Now, when we used to run continuity back in the day, um, at the peak of my, so it was, we've done software continuity programs, which if you think about click funnels, it's the continuity program.
Uh, we've done print newsletters.
I re, we published a print newsletter for about seven years, I believe, where every single month we shipped out a print newsletter.
Um, we've done membership sizes and a whole bunch of things.
And so, um, there's a lot of ways to do this.
But again, my, my favorite variation is kind of a blend of both of those.
An online component and also an offline component.
But I, I want you to understand, you don't have to do that.
These things work just as good.
If not better sometimes just having 100% online.
Okay.
Now, one of the things that we learned when we were launching our continuity programs, and this is again, more of the strategy and not something you have to do, but, but something that just a smart thing to think about is, um, continuity, it's typically a little bit harder of a sell.
Cause if you're selling a product, you're convincing someone to say, okay, this is how much this product is worth.
Right.
When you're doing your sales, finally you're saying, here's my ebook or here's my course or here's my, you know, physical product, whatever it is.
This is what it's worth.
And you got to convince somebody that that's what it's worth.
And the, and if you can show that it's worth more than, than the money for somebody than that person is going to trade you for it.
Right.
It's just a pretty simple process there.
And so that's kind of, that's kind of, uh, the, the easier way to sell something.
When you're selling continuity, you're kind of making two cells.
He wants sells that this thing that you have is a lot of value.
And second off, they have to keep paying for it every single month.
They have continued access to it.
And so typically it's a little bit harder of a sell.
Does that make sense?
You guys.
So I want you to understand, um, when you understand, uh, that part of it, um, for, for sure.
All right.
Now, um, all right.

And at the time Bill Glazer said they had 14,000 people on continuity.

So almost $700,000 a month just from this continuity program.
And what was cool is that, um, the way that Bill and Dan kind of explained it to me, which I thought was really cool.
They said that their entire business focuses around their continuity program.
Okay.
Again, this works for, for membership sites or whatever, but it focuses on continuity.
He says that, that, uh, their goal is to get all their, all their free people, all their subscribers to get into paid continuity.
And they're paid continuity.
He says basically every single month they're paying for you to be able to send them stuff and build a relationship.
And all these, these really, really cool things.
And, uh, for them, they're, again, their entire business focused around membership.
How do we get people?
How do we get them to stick?
How do we give them a good experience?
And, uh, as their membership grew, then their live events grew and then people, um, people, uh, would go back and, uh, and more people would buy their other products and other services.
And I still remember this day, Dan told me, he said that, he said that the metric they look for said for every thousand members that they have in, in continuity, that'll equal a million dollars in sales inside of their, their business.
And I was like, well, that's cool.
So I started my first goals.
If I get, I just want a thousand people on my continuity program.
And so I focused on that and we got it.
And sure enough, pretty close to almost identical to what he said, we average about a million dollars, uh, in our business from that.
And we started focusing into 2000 members and 3000 keep growing it up.
Okay.
And so, uh, that's kind of, that's kind of, uh, that, that's kind of the, the concept behind why membership is important and why, um, I think that having continuity is, um, it's, uh, it's such an important thing to figure out how to add it into your business.
And again, it doesn't have to be print newsletters like Dan's or like mine, but it does need to be something that's, that's like the glue of your business.
It's the central point.

Hey everyone, my name is Russell Brunson and I got a really cool offer for you today that I think you're really going to like.
So, um, as most of you know, uh, about a year ago, we launched a book called dot com secrets labs, one hundred day proven split test winners and went through all of the different businesses that I own and, uh, about six different markets.
We showed all of our split testing results, what worked, what didn't work.
We showed all the conversion rates.
And it was the first time I think anyone in our industry had ever actually kind of like showed everything.
Uh, most people are very, you know, I always hear marketers call like, oh, we got a 30% bump or a, you know, 30% decrease.
And they, they're always really vague with their terms where I showed all of our analytics data.
I showed the split test, what worked, what didn't work.
It just kind of gave everything.
And, uh, and I know a lot of you guys actually got that book.
If not, it's free.
Just go to dot com secrets labs.com and you can get a free copy.
It's got a hundred eight of our, of our best split tests.
But, uh, over the last year, what's been frustrating for me is that we keep testing things and we keep learning things.
All these new things that we've discovered that have been coming out.
And, uh, and I've never had a way to kind of share all this other, uh, results and, and tests and everything with anybody.
And so what I wanted to do is we wanted to actually launch a new version of our news of, of that book, but more instead of a book format where it's printed once it's kind of done, we wanted to make a monthly version where every single month I can share with you exactly what we're doing today, what split tests we ran last week and last month and what happened.

For example, right here, this is my perfect webinar script.
And some of you guys may have heard me talk about this in the past, but this is basically 10 years of me trying to figure out how to sell on a webinar and started with me, uh, one time getting invited to speak at a seminar.
I got up on stage, didn't know what I was doing and I tried to speak and I tried to sell and nobody bought anything.
It was so embarrassing.
And I was so embarrassed.
I wanted to figure out how the whole like speaking world worked and how I could sell things.
And so, uh, for over a year, um, or excuse me, for almost 10 years, I went into, I think probably eight or nine different public speaking trainings from Bill Glazers to Dan Kennedy's to Armand Moran's, uh, to Jason Flattle and it's just every single one of them and try to figure out different ways.
And, and, uh, I kept getting better and better at perfecting my, my pitch.

I tweak the script.

You get this gift and every month they have a chance to see behind the scenes of what we're doing, what's working, get our scripts, get everything.
And I think you're going to love it.
So join down below you guys and I'll see you in the first newsletter.
All right.
Do you guys like that?
I got like probably 12 of you guys asking me if you can buy it.
I got, I'm sorry.
It's not for selling out.
Hopefully next week we're going to have it live, but I want to show you that for a couple of reasons.
Okay.
There's a right way to do continuity and there's a wrong way.
Uh, the wrong way, and I see some people do this is where they'll give away.
So like, if, if I would have pitched this for, I'm like, Hey, here's this entire marketing course gift for free right here.
And then the fine print, it's like, Oh, by the way, if you forget to cancel, I'm going to bill you 49, 97 for the rest of your life.
Okay.
That's the wrong way.
A lot of people do that.
It's unethical.
You're getting customers.
Um, nothing, nothing good can come from it.
Okay.
And so I don't want to do that.
The way I do, I'm very upfront and very apparent.
Like this is a bribe.
This is not something that, you know, like this, it is what it is.
And I want to, I want to be, be very clear about that.
Okay.
Now I mentioned that because continuity can be a good thing in your business.
It can also be a bad thing if you do it wrong.
If you do it right, it's like one of the most amazing tools ever.
If you do it wrong, uh, it can destroy your business almost overnight.
In fact, uh, one of our coaching clients, I'm not going to share his name, but, uh, he, he did it the wrong way the first time he got all his merch to count shut down.
He got on a TMF list and, uh, a whole bunch of really bad things by doing it the wrong way.
Okay.
So for me, like if, and I tell people this all the time, when I look at continuity, um, I don't like this being the very first funnel that somebody's introduced to me through.
Okay.
Cause I don't like my very first transaction being like, Hey, sign up and I'm going to bill you every single month.
Cause a lot of times if you do that, it starts the relationship off on the wrong foot.
Okay.
Um, I love it as my second funnel.
So after somebody's bought a free plus shipping thing, or they bought, you know, some product from me or something like that, I love having this be the next thing where it's like, Hey, thanks so much for buying.
We know, whatever we've got this really cool monthly program that would love to have you be part of it and then push them to that.
Does that make sense?
So if you look in my funnels, uh, this is not typically the, the first funnel.
This is typically the second funnel.
Somebody will see in our sequence because I don't like leading.
I don't, I personally don't like leading with continuity.
I like leading with, um, with just, I don't know if that makes sense.
It's like, if you were to go on a girl on a date with a girl on the very first date, she like wants to go, wants to go steady with you, right?
Or whatever, whatever I've married so long, I don't even know what people call nowadays, but it's kind of weird, right?
And same thing, like if your first transaction with someone's like, Hey, pay me every month for the rest of your life.
It's just kind of weird.
And so I don't personally like it, but I love it as a second, a second thing.
He knows that my video, I talk about this thing that probably bought right ahead of time, which was the book and that's where I introduce it.
So that's just some ideas for me and some strats, strategy stuff to think about.
Okay.
Now, again, you can replicate this.
It does not have to be a print.
In fact, for mostly as I don't recommend doing a print thing.
Um, I just do, I'm just doing print cause there's a secondary strategic reasons why I'm doing it.
I like to be able to ship something to people in the mail.
I'd be able to have them financing mail going to my best customers houses.
So I can put an insert letter in there and things like that.
But I've seen people make as much as not more money to student all through online membership sites.
But I still think that if you're, if you're going to sell continuity, you've got to make the offer so good.
So like giving something cause it's just like during my membership site and every single month we're going to bill you is not a good offer saying, Hey, we've got this, this continually training.
Like for example, um, one of the people I was talking to, uh, uh, two days ago, actually on our, in our coaching program about their continuity program, they've got these really cool templates that, that they can give their people every single month.
So their continuity is every month, a new template.
I'm like, that's awesome, but not maybe we're going to want to sign up and just get a new template.
Right.
Like they'll stay on it for the template, but they need something bigger to push them over the edge because it's such a big commitment.
And so for, for her, we were talking about, um, she's got a, like a 90 minute or a, maybe a two hour long training where she goes over this whole thing and the strategy and all that stuff.
And so I'm like, that's what you're getting away for free.
It's like, Hey, I got this, you know, I, the way I would pitch it is, Hey, I've got this, this strategy is how it works.
And I just did a two and a half, three hour training, teaching this.
And I'm going to give it to you guys for free.
Um, if you join my template club, now what I'm going to be showing you the strategies, how to use these templates, you're going to need them.
Very, very badly.
But, uh, and so, you know, this is my offers.
You signed up, um, you signed up for the template club.
Um, my bribe is I'm going to give you this entire training, which is going to give you this, right?
So yeah, that's kind of how I, how I structure my things.
Is that, that helpful?
You guys, I hope.
Um, all right.
Cool.
Cool.
All right.
Cool.
So that is the continuity program and continuity.
Basically, someone signs up and they pay you every single month, tell forever.
Right.
That's the goal.
Um, and so that's, that's what a continuity program is.
Now, next thing I want to talk to you about is a concept that we call micro continuity.
And this something that, uh, five or six years ago, maybe it's longer than that.
Seven, I'm getting older.
I can't remember a few, a bunch of years ago, we kind of, we kind of create this thing out of, out of, um, kind of out of necessity.
Like we didn't really, uh, invent it on, uh, on purpose, but it just kind of happened and it was this cool thing.
And, uh, and I remember like, we discovered it one day and we created this thing.
We rolled it out and worked and like two days later, I had a, I had a workshop in our office people were coming to, and I had this other curriculum I was going to teach and I was so excited by this concept of micro continuity that we, whenever it showed up, I said, Hey guys, we're going to teach this, but I'm changing it because this is, this is what I'm excited about right now.
We taught this micro continuity concept.
And then after that, we did a product launch around it and we ended up helping so many people create micro continuity programs.
And, uh, I haven't talked much about it for a long time.
And, and, and the main reason why I never did is because, um, it was hard to create a micro continuity program from a technical standpoint, but what's cool is inside of click funnels.
It is so easy.
And so I want to, I want to explain that concept as well.
So you understand it.
And after you understand it, when I, when we log into click phones, you'll see how we execute on it and, and why it's so exciting.
So I'm actually going to open up paintbrush so I can, I can kind of map out the strategy behind how this works.
Okay.
So a micro continuity program, the way it works is somebody, you know, typically will come and they will see your video where you're selling it.
So there you are in your video.
They're going to sign up right here.
When they sign up, then they go into the members there, whatever.
But what happens is they start getting drip content, right?
So they'll get a lesson number one.
And I, all the micro continuity sites I did were always weekly.
So week one, they'd get this lesson week two, they'd get this lesson week three.
They get this lesson.
And, uh, and I would have this go out for, for a while.
Right.
So we had some micro continuity programs that were six week long programs.
We had some that were six months.
We had some that were 12 months.
We had some that were just a whole bunch of different, different things, but they were fine night.
I call micro continuity because they're micro, they're fine.
They don't go forever.
So somebody would join this membership site and say, Hey, I'm going to teach you this, this, this concept, right?
So we're going to teach you, um, for example, we're going to teach you, uh, how to use Facebook ads to drive traffic to your website.
And this is, um, a 14 week program and each week we're going to give you one piece of it and, uh, so you sign up and it's basically $30 a month for four months or whatever that is, right?
So I knew that, so they knew that it was going to end.
So it was, it was $30 a month and it lasted for four months.
Let's say it's so that, so I was going to make $120.
But for them, because they knew it was fine, it was going to end our retention rate was way higher.
Okay.
We'll find when you do a typical membership site, average retention rate on the low ends, like three months on the high ends, like eight months.
So I started getting tired of creating content.
Oh, every single month forever.
I was like, let's, let's shorten this and give everyone the same thing.
And just, you know, if they're going to stay for average five months anyway, let's just make this five months and make it a process where instead of a lot of membership sites, you log in and there's like a whole bunch of stuff.
It's kind of like walking into a library, walking, there's, wow, there's a whole bunch of stuff.
Um, and people get overwhelmed.
They don't know what to do.
And they just kind of cancel with cool, a microcontinuity program as you log in and you're just given week number one, boom.
And then week later, you get the second one and the third one and the fourth one.
And the end of it, it kind of ends.
Okay.
So that was, that's basically what a microcontinuity program is.
Just a fixed term, finite, uh, continuity program where it doesn't go forever.
It's not till death do you part.
It's just until the, the program is over.
And because we would segue, we would chop up these weekly lessons and give them one action step, one action step.
Um, people's success rate inside of these kinds of programs usually is a lot higher than a typical continuity program.
Um, anyway, so there's a lot, a lot of really good benefits of it.
So the way we always structured ours is we'd have each week, like a 15 minute video lesson and then an assignment and then it would just push for one week.
The next and just kind of pushing them through this whole process.
Um, one of my favorite success stories of microcontinuity was a girl named Joy Anderson and, uh, she was awesome.
She had, she had started, um, these daycares in her home and, and, um, turned into a really good business.
In fact, uh, I was talking to her a little while ago, she now has like four or five of these daycares that she's created.
And, uh, and a lot of people can ask her like, how do you do that?
I would love to do my own daycares.
And so she created a microcontinuity program teaching that.
And it was like, uh, things of six or eight months long, uh, coaching program that was broken down into 15 minute videos with each piece of what they needed this week.
This week you gotta do this next week.
You gotta do this.
And it was very, again, very finite, very step by step focused.
And, uh, and she had just tons of success stories with it.
She made a lot of money with it.
Well, excuse me.
So just another kind of cool way.
So does that make sense?
You guys.

Now I want to tell you guys a story about one click funnels member.
Um, um, and I'm not, I'm not condoning this.
I'm just saying this is what they did and they made a lot of money with it.
Um, is that they went and they created a course entirely of cool videos they found on YouTube.
So they didn't create any of the content themselves.
For example, let's say, like Chris Farrell here made a really quick, cool Facebook.
Traffic, tri- traffic trick here.
So here's this video.
So they just went here and came back to the members area.
And anyway, they just went and found all their favorite YouTube videos and build out an entire membership site, um, which is YouTube videos that they found.
It wasn't none of their own content.
They just, and they rationalize it.
And again, you can, if you don't agree with this, that's fine.
The way they rationalize it is like, look, all I'm doing is I'm aggregating content.
You look at what, how's the news get paid?
They aggregate content.
How do big blogs get paid?
They aggregate content.
It's like, I'm going into YouTube finding the actual videos that are a value to someone, putting them in a logical sequential order and selling access to that in the members area.
And, um, he does really, really good with that.
So this is an idea, but that's basically how it works.
Right.
Very simple.
You've got the video, plug it in here and now it works.
Now one thing that I want to make sure you guys understand, because this will drive you loony tunes.
Uh, in fact, uh, Liz called me the other day, like freaking out because she's like, I'm logging to my members area and I keep hearing my voice and it won't, it won't go away.
So when you, when somebody logs into your membership site, um, it actually, it loads all of these pages in the background.
So if you have any of these videos on any page auto-playing, even if they're not on that page, the video is going to start auto-playing.
Okay.
And so for example, if this little video right here, if I had this on.
Yeah.
So basically we should probably just take autoplay off as an option.
I had a membership site, but this is auto-playing.
Then every single person who logs in membership site, that video will auto-play no matter what page they're on, which obviously gets really annoying.
It's really bad.
So make sure just a tip, do not let your videos auto-play inside your members area.
Okay.
So that's kind of the content you guys.

Um, and one thing Todd, you want to mention, because if they are using stripe, um, membership site, they are using stripe, they have to do something different to make the recurring work for stripe?
Slightly.
So you actually set up your recurring plan inside of stripe or infusion soft.
If you're using infusion soft, so you're going to create your payment plan inside of that platform.
And then here you'll come and change it to subscription.
If you click subscription right there, Russell, um, you don't have a plan specifically for this.
These are actual billing plans, but you would click whichever plan you want to use inside of stripe and that's going to be your recurring plan.
Okay.
And, uh, for doing a micro continuity, you can see right below it.
The next option down is number of payment or payment plan payments.
So you can limit it to say three months or five months or six months, whatever you wanted to be the micro continuity range.
That's awesome.
And that's important because if you think about a stripe, they only allow you to do continuity.
So like someone signs up and then they stay on forever until they cancel.
They don't allow you to have like an end date.
And so this is a ninja thing Todd built in for me so that we could do micro continuity programs again.
And one of the other things that I'll make a note of too, right there below the stripe plan, you'll see it says note the exact plan.
They must exist in live and test mode.
So inside of stripe, they have a test mode and a live mode.
So if you're going to be running tests inside of click funnels, make sure you add the plan into both your test mode and your live mode.
That way the tests work properly.
Oh, gotcha.
Cool.
Very cool.

And I've had a couple people ask me this, like, do you only do membership sites if it's like a continuity?
My answer is no.
I created a membership site in the back of every one of my funnels because they buy something.
I want them to have it in a members area.
You know, some people were telling me that like in their sales funnel, they were setting up and just having a thank you page.
And so a lot of people somehow would find thank you page.
Some of my share with somebody else or whatever.
And so that's what I like doing.
I like doing a membership site because it locks it down.
It's only people who buy get access to it.
And it's just kind of cool.
Plus, I feel like people get a better experience when they buy something, log in there.
So they can members area as opposed to a download page where they download some stuff and they might forget where it's at and all the other issues that kind of come with that, you know, absolutely.

Is it going to push them to these download pages or push them to the membership access download page?
So it's going to push them wherever we actually set that thank you page when we were setting up the product.
And we set it for both the upsell and the order form to be the membership area.
Now, if you wanted to, you actually could set it to be a static thank you page, maybe for the upsell, since they're already getting a link to the membership area.
So if you wanted to provide more details about the upsell, you could use the upsell thank you page as a regular page instead of the membership area.
And it would still give them access to it when they log into it through their original purchase.
So that's kind of an optional step.
You could do it either way on the upsell, but for the front end product, you definitely want to use the upsell.
But for the front end product, you definitely want to set the product thank you page to the membership area.

So, but that's, that's about it, you guys.

Now, kind of the last thing I want to just kind of again, this steps back to the strategy part again, for you guys to think about is, uh, like when, like some people are like, well, does my product make sense for continuity?
Does it not?
And, and it just kind of depends.
You know, I was looking at like, if, if your product is something that somebody does once, then it doesn't make sense for continuity.
Like when we back when we were teaching microcontinuity programs, people were like launching these, these, you know, eight week, nine week, 10 week continuity programs.
And a lot of them didn't work.
And the reason we came back to it was because the thing that they were teaching somebody, some of them, some of them bought it because they wanted to learn how to do it right now.
They didn't want to wait eight weeks or 10 weeks to do.
They wanted the information right now, you know?
So if you're some, if you're selling something that's like, I'm giving access to you right now because you need, you know, you're in pain, you need this right now.
It's a one time thing that they got to figure out.
Membership is probably not good.
But if you got any kind of a continuing education or more training or things that you can keep teaching people or things like that, um, continuity is, is, uh, is obviously really, really good.
Um, also, if you go back to the product development module, we did, where we talked about how I create a system outline.
And then I go and I, I, you know, I, I do survey questions, get answers and stuff like that.
And I kind of build it out.
Um, a lot of times that the, the, the continuity of membership sites, just taking, taking that thing, like, for example, let's say you sell a book.
Okay.
In fact, this is exciting for me today.
So I'm launching my very first real book ever.
Um, we're going to try to get on the New York Times best selling list in about three months from now.
And so I got the stuff back in the editor.
So I actually printed out about a hundred copies of them.
I got one sitting here right here in my hands.
So this is like my, my book and I'm going to spend a year writing this thing.
And if I open up, I look at the table of contents.
I look at this is my system outline and the book is me teaching it through book format.
Right.
Now, if, uh, if I was going to turn this into a membership site, I would just take each of these tabs and have videos of me now teaching it and, and bulking it up.
Okay.
The system outline is the same for my front end product as my continuity.
It's just the continuity now is I'm, I'm delivering it in a different format.
Delivering it through video or through audio or for different things like that.
So a lot of times you can look at when you're existing products, you already have and then take that content, reposition it, change it around how you, how you present it and turn it into a membership site really, really easily.
We've seen tons of authors who have done that where they've taken their books and then, uh, which they were making no money on and then launching these little micro continuity or continuity programs afterwards, then are bulking up and teaching their system, the same systems in their book, but teaching it through different means and, uh, and charging, you know, 10, 20, 30 times as much as they were for their book, which is really cool.
So a lot of different ways to use it, you guys.
And I hope that the wheels in your head are spinning.
